caption:
TypeScript decorators just hit Stage 3 — no more experimentalDecorators flag needed.

These 5 patterns will clean up your codebase overnight. Which one are you adding first?

#typescript #decorators #javascript #webdev #coding #programming #developer #codingtips #cleancode #softwareengineering

slides.title: TypeScript Decorators That Clean Up Your Code
slides.subtitle: Stage 3 decorators — no experimentalDecorators needed

slides.title: @log — auto-log every call
slides.desc: Wrap any method to log its name and arguments automatically.

slides.title: @memo — cache expensive results
slides.desc: Memoize return values based on arguments.

slides.title: @sealed — freeze the class
slides.desc: Prevent adding or removing properties at runtime.

slides.title: @validate — runtime type checks
slides.desc: Validate arguments before the method runs.
slides.punchline: Catch bad inputs at the boundary.

slides.title: @throttle — rate-limit calls
slides.desc: Prevent a method from firing more than once per interval.

slides.title: Decorator Cheat Sheet
slides.question: Which decorator are you adding first? 👇

slides.teaser: Next: TypeScript infer keyword — the pattern nobody teaches
